title: sarracenia flava var. atrosanguinea
intro: Sarracenia flava var. atrosanguinea, also known as the black-veined yellow trumpet pitcher plant, is a rare and carnivorous plant species native to the southeastern United States. It contains saponins and other compounds with potential medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The plant's extracts have been traditionally used to treat various health issues, such as respiratory problems, fever, and skin conditions, due to its purported health benefits, including antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. Herbal preparations of Sarracenia flava var. atrosanguinea, often in the form of infusions or tinctures, are used in some folk medicine practices, although its efficacy and safety for human consumption have not been extensively scientifically studied.
intro_study: According to "Journal of pharmaceutical sciences", Sarracenia flava var. atrosanguinea has antitumor activity against lymphocytic leukemia P-388, which is attributed to the presence of compounds like lupeol.
